meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: HARD MANDATE (NO EXCEPTIONS): For EVERY bug/regression, you MUST (1) replicate with HIGH CONFIDENCE via a failing test, (2) validate that replication FAILS via make target, and ONLY THEN (3) apply a fix. No exceptions.
ANTI-HALLUCINATION CONSTRAINT: Do NOT invent root causes or describe nonexistent logic. If you have not read the relevant code paths, add an investigation task.
TOOLING REQUIREMENT: All verification MUST use the make-based workflow. Cross-platform checks MUST pass (make all). macOS-only integration tests via project.mk targets.
RULE OF TWO (COMMIT GATE): No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent strict reviews confirm correctness.
INDEFINITE CYCLE: The task list must never become empty. Always expand scope towards True Perfection.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Execute tasks strictly in sequence. Update blueprint.json incrementally after each task.
mandatoryDirectives.sourceOfTruth: Archive reference: scratch/archive/pr-split-v1/ (the original monolith, 6225 lines). Live chunks: internal/command/pr_split_00_core.js through pr_split_13_tui.js. Go bridge: internal/command/pr_split.go. Tests: internal/command/pr_split_*_test.go. Chunk test helper: loadChunkEngine() in pr_split_00_core_test.go. Monolith test helpers: loadPrSplitEngine()/loadPrSplitEngineWithEval() in pr_split_test.go. TUI: internal/termmux/ui/autosplit.go (AutoSplitModel). Design doc: docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md.
mandatoryDirectives.fullEndToEndIntegrationTesting: The -integration flag, -claude-command, -claude-arg flags registered in internal/command/main_test.go TestMain. project.mk targets: integration-test-prsplit, integration-test-prsplit-mcp, integration-test-termmux. New test functions in internal/command/ matching TestIntegration_* are automatically picked up by make integration-test-prsplit-mcp.
mandatoryDirectives.chunkArchitecture: 14 JS chunk files loaded in order by pr_split.go (loadChunkedScript). Each chunk is an IIFE that attaches exports to globalThis.prSplit (initialized by chunk 00). Chunks may reference symbols from earlier chunks via globalThis.prSplit. Go-injected globals (autoSplitTUI, tuiMux, splitView, planEditorFactory, prSplitConfig) are available to all chunks. Toggle via PR_SPLIT_CHUNKED=1 env var (c.useChunkedScript field in pr_split.go).

statusSection.currentState: Planning phase complete. Chunk 00 extracted and tested (~400 lines, 15+ tests via loadChunkEngine). Chunks 01-13 are stubs (~8 lines each, IIFE wrapper + TODO). Monolith functional as default (PR_SPLIT_CHUNKED=1 to use chunks). Integration tests baseline verified (make all PASSES, make integration-test-prsplit-mcp PASSES). Git clean on main.
statusSection.goalState: pr_split_script.js monolith replaced by 14 independently-testable chunk files. Each chunk is under 500 lines. Go code provides reusable modules, JS provides app-specific logic. End-to-end validation passes with REAL Claude. Terminal output is clean (no mangling). Process exits cleanly on q/Ctrl+C. make all green cross-platform. Every line has a reason to exist.

replanLog.trigger: Investigation phase: 6 regressions validated against source code
replanLog.summary: Initial blueprint rewrite with concrete file/function pointers.

replanLog.trigger: Rule of Two Review 1: 3 CRITICAL issues found
replanLog.summary: Critical fixes: (1) Regression replication tests added BEFORE each fix. (2) sendToHandle fix moved BEFORE claude command removal. (3) Existing test assertion inversions explicitly handled.

replanLog.trigger: HANA-SAN CRITICAL REVIEW: sendToHandle STILL BROKEN
replanLog.summary: T04 claimed done but ACTUAL behavior: PTY coalesces two rapid writes. Need inter-write delay. End-to-end test with REAL Claude mandatory.

replanLog.trigger: Session: Test file split + async infrastructure overhaul
replanLog.summary: pr_split_test.go split into 12 new satellite files. dispatchAwaitPromise, evalJS, evalJSAsync rewritten for proper Promise-aware dispatch.

sequentialTasks.task: R00a: Remediate global test state pollution (Git & CWD)
sequentialTasks.description: Fix atrocious test behavior where local Git HEAD detaches and os.Chdir is used without isolation. (1) Audit all tests for os.Chdir and replace with t.TempDir + proper cleanup. (2) Identify and fix the 'HEAD detachment' bug — likely caused by git commands executing in the project root instead of t.TempDir (e.g., pr_split integration tests). (3) Specifically target internal/scripting/tui_completion_*_test.go and internal/command/pr_split_integration_test.go. Use t.Setenv for PATH/GIT_CONFIG where applicable to avoid global side effects.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make all runs to completion and git status / git branch proves the repo state is EXACTLY as it was before the run. No detached HEAD. No orphaned split branches. All os.Chdir calls are wrapped in fail-safe restoration. Tests are proven to pass in clean environments (containers).

sequentialTasks.task: R00: Decouple PR split TUI from termmux package
sequentialTasks.description: Move internal/termmux/ui/ (AutoSplitModel, PlanEditor) to a dedicated UI package within internal/command (e.g., internal/command/prsplit_ui). termmux must be stripped of any UI that is not core to terminal rendering or overlays. The moved Go models must be marked as TODO for JS rewrite using the osm:bt facade. Delete any 'slop' or unrelated UI code discovered in internal/termmux.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: internal/termmux/ui is removed. pr_split.go references the new UI package. make all passes. Tests for the TUI models are updated and green. No app-specific TUI logic remains in internal/termmux. TODOs present in new Go UI package.

sequentialTasks.task: R28: Fix issues found in integration testing. Identify the issues NOT FOUND in prior regression testing.
sequentialTasks.description: It is IMPERATIVE that you assume all past work, including tests, are COMPLETE TRASH, and will fail to prove anything works correctly. With that as your basis, rigorously force each test to PROVE itself as actually validating what it needs to validate - in detail - to PROVE it is a valuable test. It is CRITICAL that the end to end behavior _works_, and it is SO FUCKING COMPLEX that you have REPEATEDLY failed to achieve this (context: `osm pr-split` is on its fourth iteration - you incompetent dog). Catch-all for bugs discovered in R26/R27, as part of this. For EACH bug: (1) Write a failing test first (per globalAlerts mandate). (2) Validate the test fails via make all. (3) Apply fix in the appropriate chunk file. (4) Validate make all passes. Common expected issues: (a) sendToHandle timing — PTY may coalesce writes. (b) Response parsing — Claude response format varies. (c) TUI callbacks — autoSplitTUI.updateProgress called with unexpected args. (d) Exit handling — goroutine leak or hung Promise. Each fix gets a regression test.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All bugs from R26/R27 resolved. Each fix has a regression test that existed before the fix was applied. make all passes. make integration-test-prsplit-mcp passes. make integration-test-prsplit passes (if Claude available). ALL tests must be excecuted with the chunked loading (no monolith). No regressions from R28 fixes. No regressions at all, on ANY platform. Verify that ALL tests TRUELY got excecuted without ANY skipping. The optional / host-dependency-having tests included, for macOS (this host has all dependencies required for all tests). Linux and Windows MUST PASS per the checks in example.config.mk
sequentialTasks.status: In Progress — Test quality audit complete. Shallow assertions strengthened (integration, validation tests). Dead PTY tests removed (2 legacy t.Skip tests, 519→146 lines). Pre-existing test timeout bugs fixed (subprocess contexts, race suppression, per-pattern termtest timeouts). make all GREEN (675s). make lint GREEN. make integration-test-vterm GREEN. Remaining: Linux container check, Windows check.

sequentialTasks.task: R28.1: Investigation: Audit termmux/TUI resizing signal flow
sequentialTasks.description: Examine internal/termmux and internal/termmux/ui/autosplit.go to identify why resize signals are missed during visibility swaps. Verify if SIGWINCH is lost when BubbleTea is in ReleaseTerminal mode or when Mux is not in Passthrough (Claude) mode. This requires checking where watchResize is instantiated and how dimensions are propagated.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Root cause identified: specific sequence where resize is ignored during swap confirmed. Audit notes added to scratch/termmux-resize-audit.md.

sequentialTasks.task: R28.2: Replicate termmux resizing failure via test
sequentialTasks.description: Create a test case in internal/termmux/termmux_test.go that simulates a terminal resize while one side is inactive/hidden, then toggles visibility. The test must fail if the newly visible side (especially Claude's PTY) uses stale dimensions. Use a mock ResizeFn to track calls.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test fails reliably when resize occurs during hidden state and toggle follows. Test name: TestMux_ResizeDuringHiddenState.

sequentialTasks.task: R28.3: Implement 'Dirty' Resize Pattern in termmux.Mux
sequentialTasks.description: Modify termmux.Mux to track the last known terminal size persistently. Introduce a mechanism to mark fixed-size components (like VTerm or the TUI) as needing a resize update if a signal was received while they were backgrounded. Ensure that when swapping to Claude, the child PTY is immediately nudged with the latest dimensions if they changed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: termmux.Mux.handleResize updates internal state and marks sides as dirty. RunPassthrough checks this state on entry. TUI receives a WindowSizeMsg (or equivalent signal) upon return if size changed. Child PTY resizeFn called on every relevant toggle.

sequentialTasks.task: R28.4: Verify termmux resizing fix and strengthen TUI resize handling
sequentialTasks.description: Run the replication test from R28.2. Additionally, ensure AutoSplitModel in internal/termmux/ui/autosplit.go correctly handles the WindowSizeMsg after a terminal restoration. Verify that no 'AI slop' was introduced in the fix.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Replication test passes. Manual verification confirms Claude and TUI resize correctly after multiple swaps and resizing at different stages. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R29: Update docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md to match final implementation
sequentialTasks.description: Review docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md against the actual extracted chunks. Update: (1) Function-to-chunk mapping — add any functions that moved between chunks during extraction. (2) Remove any functions that were culled as slop. (3) Update line counts per chunk. (4) Update the cross-chunk dependency diagram if late-binding patterns changed. (5) Add a 'Testing' section documenting loadChunkEngine usage patterns.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Every function in the doc maps to an actual export in the chunks. No phantom functions listed. Line counts within 10% of actual. Testing section shows loadChunkEngine examples for single-chunk and multi-chunk tests.

sequentialTasks.task: R30: Update CHANGELOG.md, DELETING all past `osm pr-split` content, and ONLY INCLUDING the final state, for `osm pr-split`, the actual feature. Er towards just the _features_.
sequentialTasks.description: Add entry to CHANGELOG.md after DELETING all mention of `osm pr-split` or other now-changed content. All functionality in the changelog has been added since diverging from main - as in, none of it is integrated into the trunk branch yet.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: CHANGELOG.md updated exactly per the above.

sequentialTasks.task: R31: Update docs/architecture.md and AGENTS.md
sequentialTasks.description: Update: (1) docs/architecture.md — add/update section describing chunk loading pattern. (2) AGENTS.md mandatoryDirectives.sourceOfTruth — reference '14 chunk files pr_split_00_core.js through pr_split_13_tui.js' not monolith. (3) docs/reference/command.md — update if pr-split interface changed. (4) grep -r 'pr_split_script.js' docs/ — ensure zero stale references to monolith as active implementation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Zero stale references to pr_split_script.js as active implementation in docs/ or AGENTS.md. Architecture docs accurately describe chunk loading. AGENTS.md sourceOfTruth current.

sequentialTasks.task: R32: Remove stale references across codebase
sequentialTasks.description: Comprehensive grep sweep: (1) grep -r 'pr_split_script' --include='*.go' --include='*.js' --include='*.md' excluding scratch/archive/ — must return zero. (2) grep -r 'PR_SPLIT_CHUNKED' — must return zero. (3) grep -r 'useChunkedScript' — must return zero. (4) grep -r 'loadMonolith' or similar — must return zero. For each hit: update or remove the reference.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All greps return zero results (excluding scratch/archive/ references). make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R39 — Windows + Linux CI validation (reminder - you should ALREADY have been regularly testing these platforms)
sequentialTasks.description: Verify chunks work on non-macOS: (1) make-all-in-container (Linux Docker) passes. (2) make-all-run-windows passes. Focus: path separators in dirname/fileExtension (chunk 00), git output parsing (chunks 01/05/06), shellQuote platform differences (chunk 00).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make-all-in-container passes. Windows validation passes or is documented. No platform-specific regressions.

sequentialTasks.task: R41 — Chunk architecture ADR
sequentialTasks.description: Write docs/adr/001-pr-split-chunked-architecture.md: Context (why monolith was untenable), Decision (14 IIFE chunks with sequential loading), Consequences (independent testability, loading order overhead), Alternatives (CJS modules — annoying to wire up, single-file sections — status quo). The chunking strategy is basically just a compromise to have the implementation be something 'able to be implemented from scratch without it being baked in' i.e. be indicative of what a user could have implemented as a consumer of the compiled osm binary as a custom variant, which, typically, would be easiest to distribute as a single script. Chunked script being the closes to those semantics, basically. Do note that this might be walked back in favor of a better-conceived CJS-native approach.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: ADR exists with Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives. Referenced from docs/architecture.md. Provides rationale for future maintainers.

sequentialTasks.task: W00: Termmux JavaScript Facade (Builtin)
sequentialTasks.description: Expose `internal/termmux` functionality to JavaScript so that UI components like those using `osm:bt` can be wired up with termmux directly. This facade MUST be placed in an independent `internal/builtin/termmux` package (or similar). It MUST ONLY expose the pure termmux abstractions (panes, swapping, resizing). The core `internal/termmux` package MUST NOT contain any goja bindings—these bindings must live entirely within `internal/builtin/termmux`. This ensures termmux remains transportable/independent. Note: You must evaluate signal handling and `try-lock` behavior. If the current termmux detachment behavior is flawed, refactor it here.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A JavaScript facade for termmux is implemented in `internal/builtin/termmux`. JavaScript code can orchestrate termmux behavior without polluting the core termmux module with goja bindings. All termmux bindings are localized to `internal/builtin/termmux`.

sequentialTasks.task: W01: Wizard UI Phase 1 - State Machine Design & Types
sequentialTasks.description: Design the state machine for the new guided wizard UI flow for the PR auto-split functionality in `internal/command/pr_split_13_tui.js`. MUST BE IMPLEMENTED ENTIRELY IN JAVASCRIPT, including the usage of the existing `osm:bt` (BubbleTea), `osm:bubbles`, and `osm:bubblezone` facades. Introduce structures to track distinct, resettable states: (1) Config Validating, (2) Plan Generation, (3) Plan Review, (4) Branch Building & Verifying, (5) Interactive Error Resolution, (6) Finalization. Define the pause/resume boundaries necessary allowing user interaction mid-pipeline (e.g. `AutoSplitPauseMsg`). Update `docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md` to document the new wizard phases.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JavaScript structures and state definitions for Wizard phases are implemented in the JS chunk. The `osm:bt`, `osm:bubbles`, and `osm:bubblezone` facades are correctly utilized. Architectural documentation reflects the guided wizard model.

sequentialTasks.task: W02: Wizard UI Phase 2 - Interactive Configuration & Baseline Validation
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the configuration stage in the wizard UI flow. Before generating any splits, run the verification command on the baseline branch interactively. If it fails, pause the process. Present an intuitive UI screen (built with JS and formatting facades like `osm:lipgloss`) clearly detailing that the verification mechanism is incorrectly configured or the baseline is broken. Allow the user to manually override the verification command on the spot, or abort.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: If the baseline verification command fails, the user is presented with a clear menu of options rendered via JS. The JS wizard re-evaluates the baseline upon override, proceeding only on success.

sequentialTasks.task: W03: Wizard UI Phase 3 - Interactive Plan Review Editor
sequentialTasks.description: Port the `PlanEditor` to JS (replacing `internal/termmux/ui/planeditor.go`) and wire the plan generation output into it. Use `osm:bt`, `osm:bubbles`, and `osm:bubblezone` to construct the editor view. After Claude generates the split assignment plan, automatically detect schema invalidity or glaring omissions. Regardless of validity, pause execution and render the generated plan interactively. Allow the user to intuitively request Claude to retry generation with specific text feedback, or manually override file assignments and commit messages.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The user is forced to review the generated split plan in a pure-JS TUI screen. The UI supports an 'Auto-Retry with Feedback' action and manual overrides that directly mutate the plan payload.

sequentialTasks.task: W04: Wizard UI Phase 4 - Granular Branch Check Pipeline
sequentialTasks.description: Refactor branch generation to evaluate sequentially or concurrently but with strict isolation, managed by JS chunks. For every branch chunk formed from the plan, create the branch from baseline, apply the patch, and execute the verification mechanism. Report the distinct pass/fail status to the JS UI components.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Branches are individually evaluated via JS orchestration. Output clearly denotes which branches pass validation and which contain compilation or logic errors on the JS UI. The process does not prematurely crash if one branch fails its check.

sequentialTasks.task: W05: Wizard UI Phase 5 - Interactive Error Resolution Engine
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the error resolution wizard screen for branches that fail verification, written in JS. When a branch compiling/testing fails, halt its progression and display an intuitive diff/error log over the JS TUI. Present a menu of intuitive options: [1] Auto-Resolve (send the exact test/compiler error to Claude to generate an in-place patch), [2] Manual Fix (drop to shell or await file-system changes), [3] Abort.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When a split branch evaluation fails, the JS UI pauses and offers 'Auto-Resolve'. If chosen, Claude is passed the error block and branch context, proposing a patch. The patch applies and the JS wizard retries evaluating the branch automatically.

sequentialTasks.task: W06: Wizard UI Phase 6 - End-to-End Orchestration & Summary
sequentialTasks.description: Finalize the overall JS orchestration loop and output handling. Once all branches successfully pass verification in `internal/command/pr_split_00_core.js` and subsequent chunks, summarize the outcome via the JS UI. Terminate cleanly, ensuring a validated set of branches—with proper commit messages—is present and logical. Complete any decoupling tasks (like R00) missed previously during wiring, specifically ensuring the old Go UI code is deleted in favoure of the javascript rewrite.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Running `osm pr-split` seamlessly navigates from baseline check -> plan review -> branch evaluation -> error correction -> finish, resulting in purely validated branch outputs. The flow is driven entirely by JS. Old Go TUI logic (like `internal/termmux/ui`) is successfully removed.

sequentialTasks.task: W07: Wizard E2E Integration Testing
sequentialTasks.description: Introduce end-to-end integration tests mimicking standard and hostile wizard UI flows. Create instances of `TestIntegration_WizardBaselineRetry`, `TestIntegration_WizardPlanFeedback`, and `TestIntegration_WizardAutoResolve`. Test the new JS implementation against these mocked flows.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: At least 3 complete simulation tests (happy path + 2 error-correction paths) exist and pass reliably on `make all`. All mock behavior accurately reflects reality and proves the JS implementation is robust.

sequentialTasks.task: W08: Termmux Bell Interception & OS Notification
sequentialTasks.description: Enhance the `internal/termmux` package to intercept the ASCII BELL character (\a or 0x07) emitted by multiplexed background processes. Specifically, during the `pr-split` process, if Claude finishes a task or requires attention, the user must be notified even if that terminal pane is hidden. Expose a callback mechanism for this event to the JS Termmux Facade (W00). Implement system-level notifications (e.g., utilizing `osascript`, `notify-send`, or cross-platform notification libraries) alongside a visual indicator in the active TUI to guarantee the user is alerted.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When a backgrounded process in `term-mux` emits a bell, it is successfully intercepted and logged. The user receives an OS-level desktop notification and a visual prompt on their current TUI view. The stream parsing strictly captures `\a` without corrupting ANSI sequences or standard output. Integration tests prove the bell event successfully propagates from the PTY to the JS Facade.

sequentialTasks.task: W09: Termmux Process State HUD & Visibility Overlay
sequentialTasks.description: Develop a persistent overlay/HUD in `internal/termmux` (exposed via the JS Facade) to display the real-time state of the hidden Claude process. The user must not be left blind to the state of Claude during `pr-split` operations. The HUD should prominently display metrics or indicators of activity (e.g. 'Running', 'Awaiting Input', 'Network Stalled') inferred by PTY buffer writes or process inspection, as well as an indication of whether things are working or frozen. This overlay must be visually distinct, non-obtrusive, and provide clear instructions on how to swap directly to the active process if intervention is needed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A visible HUD layer consistently renders over the active application when a background `term-mux` process is running. Process state (idle/active/blocked) is accurately reflected in the UI. If a network issue hangs the Claude process, the HUD clearly indicates a lack of activity over time, prompting the user to swap panes and investigate. Rendering is clean, without visual tearing or 'AI slop'.

sequentialTasks.task: W10: Termmux Mouse Support & Draggable UI (Investigation & Implementation)
sequentialTasks.description: Extend `internal/termmux` to handle BubbleTea mouse events (`tea.MouseMsg` exposed through JS API) to enable mouse interaction with the multiplexer UI. Implement the ability for the user to click the HUD or specific UI regions to instantly swap between the Claude terminal and the primary application view without keyboard input. Implement draggable logic allowing the user to click and reposition the HUD overlay anywhere on the screen.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: If the terminal emulator supports mouse reporting, the user can click a visual button or the HUD to toggle terminal panes. Mouse events are reliably captured and propagated without interfering with standard terminal copy/paste. The HUD overlay dynamically updates its (X, Y) rendering coordinates based on drag events, allowing the user to move it.

sequentialTasks.task: W11: Review and Verify API Tidy & Breaking Changes
sequentialTasks.description: As part of exposing the APIs to JS, take the opportunity to assess ALL internal APIs (like `termmux`). Review for rough edges, breaking changes (ensure breaking changes ARE made if they increase quality), and ensure the architecture is solid. Specifically check Mac OS integration and Windows integration for signals. All changes must be extremely robust. Note: This sits at the end of the W-series to signify a final holistic review across all the Wizard changes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All internal APIs and their bindings are clean. Integration tests prove stability across Mac OS and Windows. Breaking internal changes are embraced if they result in higher quality code.

continuousVerification.task: After EVERY significant change, run make pipeline and integration tests
continuousVerification.description: Run `make all` after every code change. Run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` after pr-split changes. Run `make integration-test-prsplit` periodically for real Claude validation. Verify chunk file sizes stay ≤ 500 lines.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix. Zero tolerance for flaky tests.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Do not claim a regression is fixed without test evidence.
No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews confirm correctness.
